cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.22)

#
# This file is generated only once,
# and is not re-generated if converter is called multiple times.
#
# User is free to modify the file as much as necessary
#

# Setup compiler settings
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_C_EXTENSIONS ON)


# Define the build type
if(NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
    set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE "Debug")
endif()
# Generate version.h at configure time (initial)
find_package(Git QUIET)

if(GIT_FOUND)
    execute_process(
        COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} describe --tags --dirty --always
        WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}
        OUTPUT_VARIABLE FW_GIT_DESCRIBE
        OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
        RESULT_VARIABLE GIT_DESCRIBE_RESULT
    )
    execute_process(
        COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} rev-parse --short HEAD
        WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}
        OUTPUT_VARIABLE FW_GIT_SHA
        OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
        RESULT_VARIABLE GIT_SHA_RESULT
    )
    if(NOT GIT_DESCRIBE_RESULT EQUAL 0)
        set(FW_GIT_DESCRIBE "unknown")
    endif()
    if(NOT GIT_SHA_RESULT EQUAL 0)
        set(FW_GIT_SHA "unknown")
    endif()
else()
    set(FW_GIT_DESCRIBE "unknown")
    set(FW_GIT_SHA "unknown")
endif()

string(TIMESTAMP FW_BUILD_TIME_UTC "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC" UTC)

message(STATUS "FW version   : ${FW_GIT_DESCRIBE}")
message(STATUS "FW git sha   : ${FW_GIT_SHA}")
message(STATUS "FW build time: ${FW_BUILD_TIME_UTC}")

configure_file(
    ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/version.h.in
    ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/generated/version.h
    @ONLY
)

# Set the project name
set(CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME openmotion-bl)

# Enable compile command to ease indexing with e.g. clangd
set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS TRUE)

# Core project settings
project(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME})
message("Build type: " ${CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE})

# Enable CMake support for ASM and C languages
enable_language(C ASM)

# Create an executable object type
add_executable(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME})

# Add STM32CubeMX generated sources
add_subdirectory(cmake/stm32cubemx)

# SECoreBin must be built first — it produces SECoreBin.bin which is embedded
# into the SBSFU firmware via .incbin in sfu_secorebin_Inc.c
add_subdirectory(SECoreBin)

# Generate sfu_secorebin_Inc.s: embeds SECoreBin.bin into the SE_CORE_Bin section.
# A dedicated assembly (.s) file is used instead of inline asm in .c because GCC
# can silently reorder or discard inline asm sections during the compile step;
# the assembler (as) processes .s files directly and honours section names reliably.
set(SE_CORE_BIN_PATH "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/SECoreBin/SECoreBin.bin")
configure_file(
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/SBSFU/App/Src/sfu_secorebin_Inc.s.in"
    "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/sfu_secorebin_Inc.s"
    @ONLY
)

# Tell CMake/Ninja that sfu_secorebin_Inc.s.obj depends on SECoreBin.bin so that
# whenever SECoreBin is rebuilt (e.g. after se_key.s changes) the embedding file
# is automatically recompiled and the correct binary is linked in.
set_source_files_properties(
    "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/sfu_secorebin_Inc.s"
    PROPERTIES OBJECT_DEPENDS "${SE_CORE_BIN_PATH}"
)

# Ensure the main target waits for SECoreBin_bin (the explicit .bin rule) to
# finish before compiling sfu_secorebin_Inc.s (Ninja parallelism guard).
add_dependencies(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} SECoreBin_bin)

# Link directories setup
target_link_directories(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    # Add user defined library search paths
)

# Collect SBSFU App sources (exclude the .c.in template)
file(GLOB SBSFU_APP_SOURCES    SBSFU/App/Src/*.c)
file(GLOB SBSFU_TARGET_SOURCES SBSFU/Target/Src/*.c)

# Add sources to executable
target_sources(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    Core/Src/led_driver.c
    Core/Src/util.c
    
    # SBSFU sources
    ${SBSFU_APP_SOURCES}
    ${SBSFU_TARGET_SOURCES}
    # SE Core binary embed (assembly file, generated by configure_file above)
    ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/sfu_secorebin_Inc.s
    # MPU SVC handler and application launch stubs
    SBSFU/App/Src/sfu_se_mpu.s
    # SE Interface sources (SE↔SBSFU API; NOT compiled into SECoreBin)
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_bootloader.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_application.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_common.c
)

# The se_interface_*.c call-gate wrappers must fit inside SE_IF_region_ROM
# (0x900 = 2304 bytes; see mapping_sbsfu.ld + the .SE_IF_Code placement in the
# linker script). At the Debug -O0 they total ~3 KB and overflow the region.
# These are thin marshalling shims with no real debug value, so build them at
# -Os (matching how ST ships the Secure Engine). -Os comes last on the command
# line and overrides the build-type -O0; -g3 is kept for line info.
set_source_files_properties(
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_bootloader.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_application.c
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core/se_interface_common.c
    PROPERTIES COMPILE_OPTIONS "-Os"
)

# Add include paths
target_include_directories(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/generated
    # Core/Inc MUST come before SECoreBin/Inc to ensure the project's
    # stm32h7xx_hal_conf.h (with all HAL modules enabled) takes precedence
    # over SECoreBin's minimal HAL config.
    Core/Inc
    # SBSFU / SE includes
    SECoreBin/Inc
    SBSFU/App/Inc
    SBSFU/Target/Inc
    # SE Core middleware (for se_interface_*.h headers)
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Core
    Middlewares/ST/STM32_Secure_Engine/Interface
    # Linker/ dir: contains mapping_export.h (referenced by se_callgate.c etc.)
    Linker
)

# Add project symbols (macros)
target_compile_definitions(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    # DEBUG_ENABLED enables SFU_DEBUG_MODE (UART4 trace output: boot banner +
    # SBSFU state-machine traces). Enabled for ALL build types so the traces are
    # present in Release too. SFU_DEBUG_MODE only gates tracing and the trace-UART
    # lifecycle; it does NOT relax any security protection (those are gated by the
    # separate SFU_*_PROTECT_ENABLE defines).
    DEBUG_ENABLED
)

# Remove wrong libob.a library dependency when using cpp files
list(REMOVE_ITEM CMAKE_C_IMPLICIT_LINK_LIBRARIES ob)

# Add linked libraries
target_link_libraries(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}
    stm32cubemx

    # Add user defined libraries
)

# Per-target linker script and library search path for SBSFU
# SECoreBin uses its own linker script defined in SECoreBin/CMakeLists.txt
target_link_options(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    -T "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/STM32H743XX_FLASH.ld"
    -Wl,-Map=${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}.map
    -Wl,--gc-sections
    -Wl,--print-memory-usage
    # The SBSFU image legitimately places executable code in a RAM-load region
    # (.RamFunc / SB_HDP_Code), which binutils >= 2.39 flags as an RWX LOAD
    # segment. This is expected for the bootloader, so silence the warning.
    -Wl,--no-warn-rwx-segments
)

# Add Linker/ to library search path so GNU ld INCLUDE directives find mapping_*.ld
target_link_directories(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/Linker"
)

# Ensure SECoreBin is fully built (including .bin post-build step) before SBSFU links
add_dependencies(${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} SECoreBin)

# Generate hex and bin files after build
add_custom_command(TARGET ${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME} POST_BUILD
    COMMAND ${CMAKE_OBJCOPY} -O ihex $<TARGET_FILE:${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}> ${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}.hex
    COMMAND ${CMAKE_OBJCOPY} -O binary $<TARGET_FILE:${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}> ${CMAKE_PROJECT_NAME}.bin
    COMMENT "Generating .hex and .bin files"
)
